_comment: Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/Tendon Transfer/ : Tendon Transfer_Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + Tendon Transfer_PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: Diploma in Orthopedics with 10 years of experience
clinical_indications: Peripheral nerve injury that has no potential to improve (e.g., root avulsions, nerve injuries not recovering after direct nerve repair or grafting, failed nerve transfers)
Peripheral nerve injuries presenting late where muscle reinnervation is impossible due to motor end-plate fibrosis
Loss of muscle or tendon following trauma
Central neurologic deficits such as spinal cord injuries and cerebral palsy
Tendon ruptures in patients with rheumatoid arthritis

preauth.label: Clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned line of management and advice for admission
Evidence of confirmed diagnosis of Leprosy (If applicable)

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical photograph of affected part confirming the diagnosis?
Evidence of confirmed diagnosis of Leprosy if applicable?

cpd_claim.q: Post-operative clinical photograph submitted showing operative outcome?
Detailed operative note includes surgical steps, findings, and implant details (if any)?
Detailed discharge summary covers post-operative course and follow-up advice?

common_queries: Clinical photograph of affected part not confirming the diagnosis or unclear
Evidence of confirmed diagnosis of Leprosy not submitted when applicable
Clinical notes missing key information (history, symptoms, findings, indication, planned management, advice for admission)
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) not submitted or incomplete
Post-op clinical photograph not submitted or unclear
Detailed operative note not submitted or lacking surgical details

clinical_key_pointers: Definition: A tendon transfer procedure relocates the insertion of a functioning muscle-tendon unit (MTU) in order to restore lost movement and function at another site.
